title: Bootstrap-sequence ABox (api-test-generator ontology, v1)
description: TBox JSON Schema for an ABox file describing the per-config bootstrap sequences of a single API. Each entry asserts: a named ordered chain of operationIds that, when run together, produce a known set of semantic types. The extractor uses these chains to seed BFS roots in the planner; the planner ranks scenarios that consume them more highly. Replaces the heuristic `RootDependencyAnalyzer` (#202). Cross-references against the bundled spec (operationIds existing, semantic types existing) are enforced by the loader at extraction time so a typo is caught at the source. The optional top-level `@context` and per-entry `@type` are JSON-LD metadata only.

@context.description: Optional JSON-LD context. Ignored by the loader; preserved verbatim so external RDF tooling can resolve term IRIs without modification.

version.description: Schema version of this ABox file. Bumped only when the TBox shape changes incompatibly.

@type.description: Optional JSON-LD type IRI. Ignored by the loader.

name.description: snake_case identifier for the sequence (used by the planner to reference contributing sequences in its scoring rationale). Must be unique within the ABox.

items.description: operationId of an operation in the bundled spec. Sequences whose operationIds are not all present in the spec are silently dropped at load time so the same ABox can ship across API variants that omit some operations.
Semantic-type name produced by running this sequence end-to-end. Must exist in the spec's semantic-kinds registry.
